articles.title: Windows 11 is getting a macOS-like speed boost
articles.description:
Microsoft is currently testing a new speed boost feature in Windows 11 that is designed to improve app launch times and make things like the Start menu feel more responsive. The feature, which is reportedly called "Low Latency Profile," will ramp up CPU frequency in short bursts to improve the speed of menus, flyouts, apps, and more - much like how macOS handles similar tasks.
Windows 11 testers have been trying out the new unannounced feature over the past week, and noticing significant speed improvements launching File Explorer or the Start menu, as well as apps like Outlook, the Microsoft Store, and Paint.


TESTED: Windows 11's upcoming …


Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Forza Horizon 6 has been leaked and cracked a week before its release
articles.description:
The full version of Playground Games' upcoming Forza Horizon 6 has leaked online over the weekend and has already been cracked by pirates. The download appeared on file sharing sites after some Steam users were allegedly able to obtain access to an unencrypted preload version of the game.
Forza Horizon 6 isn't supposed to launch until May 19th on Xbox Series S / X and PC, but cracks to bypass online checks in the game are already widely available. Reddit users first noticed that games files, totaling more than 150GB, had appeared online yesterday, before Reddit's legal operations team removed the post.
Earlier this year a pre-release versi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Logitech’s tiny folding mouse improves upon the laptop trackpad
articles.description:
You might think this looks like a phone at first glance, but it isn’t — it’s a mouse that’s folded in half. | Images by Logitech / shared by WinFuture	

Logitech is reportedly developing a new wireless mouse that folds in half to make it easier to carry around in a bag or pocket. According to leaked marketing images shared by WinFuture, Logitech's foldable mouse caused "22 percent less muscle strain" compared to using a laptop trackpad, and can be used across "multiple operating systems."
Logitech's design is visually similar to Microsoft's Surface Arc mouse and Lenovo's Yoga mouse, sporting the same arched shape when unfolded for use. One key difference is that while Microsoft and Lenovo's offerings can only be folded flat, the new Logitech mouse - the name of which is still unknown - fold …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The Bastl Kalimba is a wild synth that thinks it’s a thumb piano
articles.description:
It’s already raised over $700,000 on Kickstarter. | Image: Bastl	

Make no mistake, the Bastl Kalimba is a synthesizer, you just play it like a kalimba. Its tines don't really make much sound. There is an internal mic that you can blend in for a little acoustic spice, but it's mostly driven by the synth engine that combines physical modeling and FM. The tines are actually touch and velocity-sensitive triggers. And, while it can sound somewhat like a real kalimba, it's a lot more sonically versatile and offers features you can only find on a synth.
Beyond the synth sounds that range from pluck to pads, there are also built-in effects covering basic spatial effects like delay and reverb, as well as distortio …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Ashnymph’s Childhood EP is exhilarating dance goth rock
articles.description:
I can’t wait for a proper full length. | Image: Blitzcat Records	

I've got to thank my oldest friend and concert buddy, Tim, for turning me on to this one. Ashnymph is a London band that blends post-punk melodies with Krautrock rhythms and industrial grime. Their debut EP, Childhood, drifts between dreamy vocals buried in layers of reverb and four-on-the-floor dancefloor pounding. It's a thrilling opening salvo from a band that feels on the cusp of a major breakthrough.
Childhood opens with an ambient recording of someone walking down a hall (I think), and some swirling synth noise before the first song, "Island in the Sky" kicks off properly with a motorik beat and bass throb. The thin, digitally manipul …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Cricut’s $99 craft cutting machine helped me feel creative again
articles.description:
The desk-friendly Cricut Joy 2 comes in multiple colors. | Photo: Sheena Vasani / The Verge	

I've always been skeptical of products that claim to help you lead a more creative life. But one recently won me over. I've spent three weeks with the Cricut Joy 2, a smart cutting and drawing machine that made it easy for me to get back into making stickers, cards, bookmarks, and more.
The small $99 gadget isn't perfect, but its capabilities and app-provided templates were just what I was looking for. Caregiving, self-criticism, and mental health struggles have made it tough to tinker and doodle like I used to, and yet the Joy 2 clicked with me. It doles out quick wins when you're in the mood to create.
I've been testing the Cricut Joy 2  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: ‘Truly terrifying’: Alberta voter data breach raises fears for Canada’s electoral integrity
articles.description:
Debates over secession overshadowed by revelations separatist-linked group gained access to list of electors
The illegal use of voter information by rightwing separatists in the province of Alberta has raised fresh fears over Canada’s electoral integrity by making valuable and “incredibly confidential” personal data easily accessible to malicious actors, security experts have warned.
The data breach, one of the largest in Canadian history, has prompted warnings of a “truly terrifying” new battleground over information, persuasion and foreign interference in already weakened democratic systems.

articles.title: Windows 11 is getting a macOS-like speed boost
articles.description:
Microsoft is currently testing a new speed boost feature in Windows 11 that is designed to improve app launch times and make things like the Start menu feel more responsive. The feature, which is reportedly called "Low Latency Profile," will ramp up CPU frequency in short bursts to improve the speed of menus, flyouts, apps, and more - much like how macOS handles similar tasks.
Windows 11 testers have been trying out the new unannounced feature over the past week, and noticing significant speed improvements launching File Explorer or the Start menu, as well as apps like Outlook, the Microsoft Store, and Paint.


TESTED: Windows 11's upcoming …


Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Forza Horizon 6 has been leaked and cracked a week before its release
articles.description:
The full version of Playground Games' upcoming Forza Horizon 6 has leaked online over the weekend and has already been cracked by pirates. The download appeared on file sharing sites after some Steam users were allegedly able to obtain access to an unencrypted preload version of the game.
Forza Horizon 6 isn't supposed to launch until May 19th on Xbox Series S / X and PC, but cracks to bypass online checks in the game are already widely available. Reddit users first noticed that games files, totaling more than 150GB, had appeared online yesterday, before Reddit's legal operations team removed the post.
Earlier this year a pre-release versi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

topStories.description: "This revised timeline gives us the best opportunity to build a world-class competition that New Zealanders can be proud of and that fans deserve"

topStories.title: GM agrees to pay $12.75 million to settle California lawsuit over misuse of customers' driving data
topStories.description: General Motors collected driving data through OnStar and sold it to data brokers.

topStories.title: Taiwan fears Trump will speak off-script on its fate in Beijing
topStories.description: Taiwanese officials fear even the most subtle rhetorical change in policy from Trump could imperil a delicate status quo that has held for decades.

topStories.title: Cartel wars, El Chapo's sons, a mysterious kidnapping and an extraordinary U.S. indictment
topStories.description: The U.S. case against a Mexican governor is entwined with the mysterious kidnapping of Sinaloa cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada. That betrayal may have triggered a political and criminal reckoning on both sides of the border.

topStories.title: Big donors backed Harris in 2024. For 2028, they're not so sure
topStories.description: Though few in the party want to criticize Harris, few appear ready to endorse her. Conversations about her prospects often come down to one thing: Democrats' anxiety about winning.

topStories.title: BTS came to Mexico and these fans will never be the same
topStories.description: More than 1. million Mexicans tried to get tickets to this week's BTS shows in Mexico City. Many of those who failed showed up anyway.

topStories.title: California abortion pill suppliers ready with workaround in case of Supreme Court ban
topStories.description: The U.S. Supreme Court is weighing whether to restrict access to the country's most popular abortion method, but California's network of online providers and pharmaceutical suppliers already has an alternative in mind.

topStories.title: A vast dam across the Bering Strait could stop the AMOC collapsing
topStories.description: If a key ocean current collapses it could plunge northern Europe into a big freeze. Now researchers are weighing up a drastic intervention – building a 130-kilometre-wide dam between the US and Russia

topStories.title: Trump reportedly plans to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
topStories.description: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

dailyNews.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
dailyNews.description: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to allow people to install plug-in solar systems, often called balcony solar. These small arrays require little to no setup and could help cut emissions and power bills. Balcony solar is already popular in Europe, and proponents say that the systems could make solar power more accessible for…

dailyNews.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
dailyNews.description: Health officials have identified at least 10 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

dailyNews.description: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

dailyNews.description: Motorcycle dangles from traffic light after collision

dailyNews.title: Florida’s Ailing $6 Billion Rail Line Has Debt Vultures Circling
dailyNews.description: It’s becoming clearer by the day that Brightline, the struggling Florida private railroad, is shaping up to rank among the biggest municipal-bond restructurings ever, alongside the likes of Puerto Rico and Detroit.

dailyNews.title: WWE legend The Rock roasts Draymond Green, says he has the 'laziest' Black name
dailyNews.description: Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson torched Draymond Green at Netflix's Kevin Hart roast, mocking his name and suggesting the Warriors star should retire.

dailyNews.title: 17 US citizens repatriated from Spain after Hantavirus outbreak aboard cruise ship
dailyNews.description: Seventeen US citizens were repatriated from a cruise ship after one tested positive for the Andes strain of Hantavirus. Two were transported in biocontainment units, and upon arrival in Omaha, they will receive medical evaluation and monitoring.

dailyNews.title: Hengli’s Former Singapore Arm Dismisses Staff After US Sanctions
dailyNews.description: Hengli Petrochemical International Pte dismissed some Singapore-based staff, according to people with knowledge of the matter, weeks after its then-parent was slapped with US sanctions.

dailyNews.title: Simple body movement could help the brain 'clean itself,' researchers find
dailyNews.description: Penn State researchers found that abdominal muscle contractions during movement push cerebrospinal fluid through the brain, flushing out waste.

dailyNews.title: We will all pay for the Democrats’ antitrust crusade that killed Spirit Airlines
dailyNews.description: Democrats blocked the Spirit Airlines and JetBlue merger, killing 17,000 jobs and eliminating a budget carrier as summer airfares surge 18% over 2025.

dailyNews.description: The war in Iran, trade, artificial intelligence and Taiwan are expected to be on the agenda. But expectations are modest.

dailyNews.description: Plus, how pharma swag became “one of the white whales of thrifting.”

dailyNews.title: They Were Promised New Septic Tanks. Trump Called It ‘Illegal DEI.’
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department ended a deal that had helped fund a solution to the sewage crisis in rural Alabama. “Almost like we are starting all over again,” one activist said.

dailyNews.title: Scientists Press Congress on Dismissal of National Science Foundation Board and Research Funding
dailyNews.description: The slowed pace of grants by the National Science Foundation, under attack from the White House, could put the United States at a disadvantage with China, the scientists warned.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine Looks to Step Away From Trump and U.S.
dailyNews.description: With peace talks on ice and Ukraine now more self-reliant, President Volodymyr Zelensky seems to be stepping away from the United States.

dailyNews.title: The families going hungry because of Trump’s food stamp cuts
dailyNews.description: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.

dailyNews.title: The cage match is on for tickets to Trump’s UFC fight at the White House
dailyNews.description: Some 4,000-plus spectators who are lucky enough, cunning enough or rich enough will score a seat to the UFC fight on the South Lawn next month.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
dailyNews.description: President Trump appeared to reject Iran’s response to the latest U.S. peace proposal to end the war, calling the Iranian demands “totally unacceptable.” Iranian state media reported the response focuses on opening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, but does not mention nuclear weapons. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump Heads out on High-Stakes China Trip Amid Iran War
dailyNews.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to discuss President Donald Trump’s high-stakes trip to meet with China’s Xi Jinping for the first time since the Iran war began. “China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil and has been backing Iran in this conflict. So to that end, Mr. Trump is expected to press Beijing to scale back that support and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz,” Kristen says.

dailyNews.title: Arizona food banks swamped by SNAP cuts
dailyNews.description: Nearly a year after President Donald Trump signed his “big, beautiful bill” into law, millions are losing SNAP benefits – and Arizona is becoming one of the clearest examples of the fallout. NBC News’ Shannon Pettypiece reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Iran's response to peace proposal "totally unacceptable"
dailyNews.description: President Trump didn't provide details on the issues he had with the response or what would come next.
